title: Getting Started
text: example object contains: cells: one row per cell cell, sample, annotation embedding: two-dimensional coordinates expr: sparse count matrix
code: library(scdown)  example_data <- scdown_example() str(example_data, max.level = 1) #> List of 3 #>  $ expr     :Formal class 'dgCMatrix' [package "Matrix"] with 6 slots #>  $ cells    :'data.frame':   108 obs. of  3 variables: #>  $ embedding:'data.frame':   108 obs. of  3 variables:

title: Getting Started
code: obj <- scdown(   example_data,   annotation_col = "annotation",   sample_col = "sample" )  obj #> <scdown_obj> #>   cells: 108  #>   genes: 21  #>   annotations: 6  #>   samples: 3  #>   value type: counts

title: Getting Started
code: res <- run_core(   obj,   signatures = c("cytotoxic", "antigen_presentation") )  names(res) #> [1] "summary"            "map"                "markers"            #> [4] "annotation_check"   "average_expression" "cluster_similarity" #> [7] "signatures"         "communication"

title: Getting Started
code: head(res$annotation_check$tables$annotation_panel_scores) #>   annotation score_name  score #> 1     B_cell     b_cell 0.9750 #> 2      CD4_T     b_cell 0.5250 #> 3      CD8_T     b_cell 0.4750 #> 4 Macrophage     b_cell 0.5625 #> 5   Monocyte     b_cell 0.4500 #> 6         NK     b_cell 0.5250

title: Getting Started
code: head(res$average_expression$tables$average_expression) #>   annotation gene mean_expression #> 1      CD4_T CD74        0.000000 #> 2      CD4_T CD3D        7.552637 #> 3      CD4_T CCR5        6.860015 #> 4      CD4_T PRF1        0.000000 #> 5      CD4_T CCL5        0.000000 #> 6      CD4_T APOE        0.000000 head(res$cluster_similarity$tables$cluster_similarity) #>   annotation_1 annotation_2 correlation #> 1       B_cell       B_cell  1.00000000 #> 2        CD4_T       B_cell  0.41803263 #> 3        CD8_T       B_cell -0.03177355 #> 4   Macrophage       B_cell  0.60050246 #> 5     Monocyte       B_cell  0.57845072 #> 6           NK       B_cell  0.41803263

title: Getting Started
code: head(res$signatures$tables$signature_score_by_annotation) #>   annotation           score_name  score #> 1     B_cell antigen_presentation 0.8750 #> 2      CD4_T antigen_presentation 0.2375 #> 3      CD8_T antigen_presentation 0.1750 #> 4 Macrophage antigen_presentation 0.5625 #> 5   Monocyte antigen_presentation 0.4125 #> 6         NK antigen_presentation 0.2375

title: Getting Started
code: head(res$communication$tables$communication_pair_summary) #>     sender receiver     score #> 1    CD8_T    CD4_T 24.869376 #> 2 Monocyte    CD4_T  8.870093 #> 3       NK    CD4_T 24.869376 #> 4    CD8_T    CD8_T 24.869376 #> 5 Monocyte    CD8_T  8.870093 #> 6       NK    CD8_T 24.869376

title: Getting Started
text: report writes one HTML file plus CSV PNG artifacts section.
code: report_path <- build_scdown_report(obj) report_path #> [1] "C:\\Users\\daiki\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\RtmpoVBCfw\\scdown-report-3d8c96931c9/scdown_report.html"

title: Authors and Citation
text: User, D. (2026). scdown: Simple Downstream Analysis Cell Annotation. R package version 0.4.2. https://github.com/dai540/scdown
code: @Manual{,   title = {scdown: Simple Downstream Analysis After Cell Annotation},   author = {Daiki User},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.4.2},   url = {https://github.com/dai540/scdown}, }

title: scdown
text: scdown minimal downstream package annotated single-cell RNA-seq. assumes QC, normalization, clustering, annotation already done. package intentionally narrow focuses five practical jobs inside one dataset: cluster annotation maps marker discovery annotation checks known marker panels cluster-level expression signature summaries exploratory ligand-receptor interaction ranking Website: https://dai540.github.io/scdown/

title: scdown
text: Install GitHub pak: remotes: install source tarball: load package:
code: install.packages("pak") pak::pak("dai540/scdown") install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("dai540/scdown") install.packages("path/to/scdown_<version>.tar.gz", repos = NULL, type = "source") library(scdown)

title: scdown
text: scdown() accepts: long-format table cell, gene, value, annotation columns list expr, cells, optional embedding SingleCellExperiment Seurat object minimal metadata columns : cell annotation sample available

title: scdown
code: library(scdown)  obj <- scdown(   scdown_example(),   annotation_col = "annotation",   sample_col = "sample" )  res <- run_core(   obj,   signatures = c("cytotoxic", "antigen_presentation") )  build_scdown_report(obj)

title: scdown
text: scdown() summarize_dataset() plot_map() find_markers() check_annotation() plot_average_expression() plot_cluster_similarity() plot_signature() infer_communication() run_core() build_scdown_report()

title: scdown
text: Typical outputs include: cluster map PNGs marker tables annotation check tables heatmaps average-expression summaries cluster-similarity heatmaps signature score tables communication ranking tables one compact HTML report

title: scdown
text: scdown try replace upstream specialist tools. QC, ambient RNA correction, doublet detection, normalization, integration, clustering. group-aware differential abundance differential expression. claim publication-grade communication inference. Use Seurat, scater, scuttle, scran, SingleR, muscat, dreamlet, miloR, CellChat stages.

text: Home: https://dai540.github.io/scdown/ Getting Started: https://dai540.github.io/scdown/articles/getting-started.html Reference: https://dai540.github.io/scdown/reference/index.html News: https://dai540.github.io/scdown/news/index.html

title: scdown
text: R/object.R: input standardization R/downstream.R: minimal downstream workflow R/resources.R: built-signatures, marker panels, ligand-receptor pairs R/report.R: compact HTML reporting vignettes/: getting-started tutorial

what: Build a compact HTML report — build_scdown_report
title: Build a compact HTML report — build_scdown_report
Build a compact HTML report — build_scdown_report
code: build_scdown_report(   obj,   signatures = c("cytotoxic", "antigen_presentation"),   panels = c("t_cell", "nk", "b_cell", "monocyte", "macrophage", "dendritic"),   signature_sets = NULL,   marker_panels = NULL,   outdir = tempfile("scdown-report-") )

title: Build a compact HTML report — build_scdown_report
text: obj scdown_obj. signatures Character vector built-signature names named list signatures. panels Character vector built-marker panel names named list marker panels. signature_sets Optional named list custom signatures. marker_panels Optional named list custom marker panels. outdir Output directory.

title: Build a compact HTML report — build_scdown_report

what: Find marker genes for each annotation — find_markers
title: Find marker genes for each annotation — find_markers
Find marker genes for each annotation — find_markers
code: find_markers(   obj,   top_n = 10L,   exclude_pattern = "^(RPL|RPS|MT-|MALAT1$|MTRNR|HSP)",   min_pct_in = 0.1,   min_pct_diff = 0.05,   min_marker_score = 0.25,   outdir = NULL )

title: Find marker genes for each annotation — find_markers
text: obj scdown_obj. top_n Number top marker genes per annotation. exclude_pattern Pattern low-information genes exclude. min_pct_in Minimum detection rate inside target annotation. min_pct_diff Minimum detection improvement rest. min_marker_score Minimum expression improvement rest. outdir Optional output directory.

what: scdown: Minimal downstream analysis after cell annotation — scdown-package
title: scdown: Minimal downstream analysis after cell annotation — scdown-package
text: scdown compact downstream toolkit annotated single-cell RNA-seq data goal understand structure within one dataset. keeps workflow short focused :

title: scdown: Minimal downstream analysis after cell annotation — scdown-package
text: cluster annotation maps marker discovery marker-based annotation checks average expression summaries cluster similarity signature scoring exploratory cell-cell communication compact HTML reporting intended annotation-interpretation, group-aware differential testing full upstream preprocessing.

title: scdown: Minimal downstream analysis after cell annotation — scdown-package
Create a scdown object — scdown
text: scdown() standardizes annotation-single-cell inputs compact object cluster interpretation within one dataset. Supported inputs :

title: Create a scdown object — scdown
code: scdown(   x,   annotation_col = "annotation",   sample_col = "sample",   reduced_dim = "UMAP",   embedding = NULL,   assay_name = NULL,   expression_layer = NULL,   signatures = NULL,   marker_panels = NULL,   lr_pairs = NULL )

title: Create a scdown object — scdown
text: x Input data. annotation_col Column containing cluster annotation labels. sample_col Optional column containing sample identifiers. reduced_dim Reduced-dimension name used extracting embeddings Bioconductor Seurat objects. embedding Optional data frame cell, UMAP1, UMAP2. assay_name Optional assay name SingleCellExperiment Seurat. expression_layer Optional Seurat layer slot name. signatures Optional named list custom signatures. marker_panels Optional named list custom marker panels. lr_pairs Optional data frame ligand, receptor, pathway.

title: Create a scdown object — scdown
text: long table cell, gene, value list expr, cells, optional embedding SingleCellExperiment Seurat object

title: scdown 0.5.1
text: removed non-package GitHub issue, PR, release automation files keep repository focused R package simplified .Rbuildignore .gitignore rebuilt pkgdown pruning repository-files

title: scdown 0.5.0
text: simplified package annotation-, within-dataset downstream analysis removed group-aware tests, handoff helpers, composition summaries, pipeline guidance focused API maps, markers, marker-panel annotation checks, average expression, cluster similarity, signature scoring, exploratory communication, compact reporting cleaned documentation, vignette, tests, generated site around smaller scope

title: scdown 0.4.2
text: fixed GitHub release workflow installing vignette-building dependencies R CMD build added manual dispatch support release workflow release can rerun without creating another tag first

title: scdown 0.4.1
text: added release workflow can publish GitHub releases pushed version tags expanded CI single Linux check multi-OS matrix extra devel check Linux split strengthened tests around sparse inputs, failure modes, report output, handoff schemas added repository-facing metadata files CITATION.cff issue templates improve public package trust signals clarified HTML report built-tests screening-oriented hand specialist methods primary claims

title: scdown 0.4.0
text: added scope_table() make intended boundary scdown explicit added export_for_handoff() prepare objects muscat, dreamlet, miloR, CellChat workflows expanded build_scdown_report() scope guidance plus marker communication test sections strengthened README, pkgdown, recommended pipeline vignette around exploratory-vs-specialist handoff

title: scdown 0.3.0
text: clarified scdown annotation-downstream hub rather full upstream workflow added recommended pipeline helper handoff vignette PBMC, tumor immune, patient comparison use cases updated README pkgdown site explain scdown fits specialist tools use

title: scdown 0.2.0
text: switched sparse-first internal matrix representation added custom signatures, marker panels, lineage mapping, ligand-receptor pairs split exploratory functions sample-aware testing functions added CI workflows build hygiene files
